title: About Evas and their behaviors
wikitext:
[]: About Evas and their behaviors             Quote Post by ProbsT » Sun Dec 09, 2012 11:46 pm I rewatched the series some days ago and I don't remember of anything clarifying the will and duality of the Evangelions. Even with they being restraint by the bindings, sometimes they act individually, like when Eva-01 tried to protect Shinji in the first episode (if my memory doesn't fails me). It's clear in this moment that Eva-01 was trying to save Shinji from iminent death (clearly because he is Yui's son). This act of protection continues in other episodes, for example, when the same Eva enters in Berserk mode. Ok until here. But, there're moments where the Evas act like they're a little irrational, in an opposite way. If there's a human soul in the Evas, why they doesn't adopt a more rational behavior? I mean, the Evas are like "giant humans", aren't they? Why to they have to act with so much savagery, like when Eva-01 consumed Zeruel? Why do they seem "invaded" sometimes when they lost control and consequently reject the pilot (like in the Rei's incident where Gendo saves her)? It's like inside them there's a constant antithesis (or they're very bipolar), because sometimes they act with humanity, and other times with savagery. I don't know if I can make a reasonable explanation for the Eva's agressive behavior, but it seems that the souls inside them are disturbed being held and controlled. And, consequently, they get pretty nervous when Nerv loses control upon them. Anyone here have a better explanation for their behavior? I think that I lost an important detail here. Furthermore, despite all the agressiveness and protection instincts, is there a possibility that each Evangelion possesses a will? I think it'll be rather hard to discuss about this, because at the same time we're analising a human behavior, we're analising something strange and almost totally unknown. This is my first topic in a forum, so I'm pretty nervous here. I don't know if I'm in doubt with something so obvious that only I didn't notice. I don't know too if I'm doing the right thing opening this subject, or if this is all a meaningless anxiety. Well, I hope you'll understand what I'm trying to say.

[]:             Quote Post by pioneer » Mon Dec 10, 2012 12:44 am Have you ever heard of moms getting a rush of adrenaline and suddenly being able to pick up cars to grab their baby who rolled under there? I think 01 moving in the first episode is like that type of situation. As someone pointed out in another thread, Yui probably went nuts and ate Zeruel like that because she's trapped in a cage for years and can't interact with anyone in a meaningful way. Also, she has to put up with Shinji being emo inside of her head, dealing with that would make me eat someone's face.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Mon Dec 10, 2012 1:40 am I do not find the contradictions contradictory. When souls are infused into the Evangelion I imagine that there are many issues regarding neurology, such as that the Evangelion would likely lack the structure and memories of the brain the soul was used to. Also, an Evangelion is anything but pleasant to live as, given that one is paralyzed and bound in the most cruel way with a massive machine unnaturally inserted into one's spine. This torture would certainly result in insanity in any human. In addition, the Evangelion most likely, like other angels, has a very different and alien mental structure compared to ours. What exactly do you mean by "is there a possibility that each Evangelion possesses a will?" Do you mean to ask if the Evangelion, independently of the implanted human soul, posesses a soul, or at least animalistic instincts? I think that it is known that an Evangelion by birth lacks a soul, though as for some form of instinctual behavior I do not know.

[]:             Quote Post by Sgt. Griff » Mon Dec 10, 2012 2:08 am Eva behaviour I would assume just reflects the behaviour of the souls trapped within, the analogy about mothers and adrenaline mentioned above is especially relevant(However untrue the actual event is) as the souls are generally the souls of the mother, with a few possible exceptions and one definite exception. So yeah, it's mama bear protecting baba bear except mama bear is fifty metres tall and incredibly ridiculously strong.

[]: Re: About Evas and their behaviors             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 10, 2012 3:10 am ProbsT wrote: This is my first topic in a forum, so I'm pretty nervous here.

[]: Re: About Evas and their behaviors             Quote Post by ProbsT » Mon Dec 10, 2012 1:41 pm What exactly do you mean by "is there a possibility that each Evangelion possesses a will?" Do you mean to ask if the Evangelion, independently of the implanted human soul, posesses a soul, or at least animalistic instincts?

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Mon Dec 10, 2012 6:37 pm It could also be like Bruce Banner described being the Hulk in the movie (the good one with Ed Norton). Your concious mind is buried under so much instinct and sensation that higher thought processes go right out the window.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Mon Dec 10, 2012 6:55 pm ProbsT wrote: Something this way. I mean, does Yui have a particular will or a desire even being imprisioned in an Evangelion? Like the will of Gendo in the Instrumentality of reunite with her. I'm pretty curious about this. Excluding the fact that the evas are pieces for a prophecy, being almost fully controlled by Nerv, I think that in the depths of their souls must have something more than simply salvagery and maternal relationship with the pilots.

[]:             Quote Post by ProbsT » Mon Dec 10, 2012 7:59 pm Lavinius wrote: Note that the fact your misspelling(?) of savagery as salvagery could cause great confusion, as the act of retreiving a soul is known as salvaging.
